excerpt: The public executable is pptx, but the Python package is scaffolded as pptx_cli. This is intentional. python-pptx already uses the import namespace pptx, so reusing that module name here would create a collision.
Keep the Python package name as pptx_cli. Do not introduce a Python package named pptx; it collides with python-pptx.
The pptx_cli package name avoids collision with the dependency namespace used by python-pptx.
Public CLI name must remain pptx.
Keep the public executable name as pptx.

ref_title: PRD.md - FR-15: Return a structured response envelope
excerpt: The system shall support a structured response envelope for every command in machine-readable mode. The envelope shall include, at minimum: schema_version, request_id, ok, command, result, warnings, errors, metrics. On failure, result shall be null rather than omitted.

ref_title: CLI-MANIFEST.md - Every Command Returns a Structured Envelope
excerpt: The single most important rule. Every command, success or failure, returns the same top-level JSON shape. Agents parse one schema, not N.
machine-readable responses use one stable envelope shape

ref_title: PRD.md - FR-16: Emit machine-readable errors and stable exit codes
excerpt: The system shall emit structured errors with stable machine-readable error codes, human-readable messages, and optional structured details.
Additive changes are preferred over silent contract changes; do not rename error codes or exit-code categories casually.
Keep codes stable across versions. Add new ones freely; never rename or remove.

ref_title: PRD.md - Error handling and policy modes
excerpt: Recommended exit-code categories: 0 success, 10 validation or schema error, 20 permission or policy failure, 40 conflict or stale-state failure, 50 I/O or packaging failure, 90 internal error.

ref_title: CLI-MANIFEST.md - Exit Codes Are a Contract
excerpt: Map error categories to distinct exit codes so shell scripts and CI pipelines can branch without parsing JSON. 0 Success, 10 Validation error, 20 Permission denied, 40 Conflict, 50 I/O error, 90 Internal error.

ref_title: AGENTS.md - What not to change casually
excerpt: exit-code mapping
manifest facts and human annotations must remain layered, not conflated
manifest facts and annotations are separate layers
The v1 storage model for these annotations shall be a separate annotations.yaml file layered on top of manifest.yaml.
write operations must support --dry-run

ref_title: PRD.md - FR-18: Support dry-run for mutating commands
excerpt: The system shall support --dry-run on mutating commands that create or modify on-disk artifacts. Dry-run responses shall include a structured change summary describing the outputs that would be created or modified.
Every command that changes state must support --dry-run. Execute the full pipeline, return what would change, write nothing.
stdout must remain parseable in machine-readable mode
stdout is for structured data. Everything else is noise that burns tokens. stdout is exclusively for the structured response (JSON). One object, no preamble, no epilog.

ref_title: PRD.md - FR-21: Support agent-oriented output controls
excerpt: JSON output intended for machine parsing on stdout. progress and diagnostics on stderr.

ref_title: PRD.md - FR-20: Use safe file-write semantics
excerpt: The system shall write generated artifacts using safe file-write semantics, including temporary-file staging and atomic replacement where the operating system and output type allow it.
safe file-write semantics are required for mutating commands
preserve safe temp-file and atomic-write semantics
treat input .pptx, YAML, and JSON files as untrusted. avoid writing outside explicit output directories. validate paths and archive members before extraction.

Security concerns for this project may include: malicious .pptx package parsing behavior, zip bombs or oversized package handling, path traversal during archive extraction, unsafe file-write behavior or overwrite risks.
src/pptx_cli/commands/ owns individual command implementations and thin orchestration logic
Keep command modules thin and move reusable logic into core/
Use typed models for contracts before introducing ad hoc dictionaries that leak across modules.
Prefer typed models for shared contracts

ref_title: PRD.md - Principle 6.1: Preserve, do not imitate
excerpt: The engine should preferentially preserve and clone template assets from the original .pptx rather than restyle content from scratch.
clone approved layout structures. map content into approved placeholders. preserve masters, themes, and protected elements.
Each approved slide layout is a typed contract with named placeholders, geometry, allowed content types, and optional rules.
only approved layouts can be used. only declared placeholders can be filled. content types must match placeholder types.
Do not change CLI command names, response envelope fields, error-code taxonomy, or exit-code mapping without updating docs and tests in the same change.

ref_title: PRD.md - FR-17: Provide a machine-readable guide command
excerpt: The system shall provide a guide command that returns the CLI command catalog, input and output schema references, error code taxonomy, exit-code mapping, identifier conventions, and representative examples.
command discovery is available through pptx guide

ref_title: CLI-MANIFEST.md - Progressive Discovery via Built-In Guide
excerpt: Agents should not need external docs. Build a machine-readable guide command that returns the full CLI schema in one call.
Given the same manifest, content payload, and CLI version, output should be reproducible.
determinism

ref_title: TESTING.md - What every PR must test
excerpt: happy path for the changed behavior. failure path with the expected error code or validation response. machine-readable output shape if command behavior changes.
PyPI distribution name should remain pptx-cli unless an explicit migration plan is documented
The tool should never silently degrade fidelity. When exact preservation is not possible, it should warn or fail according to policy.
wrapper generation remains thin and delegates to the shared engine
The v1 wrapper output shall be a Python package scaffold that exposes template-specific commands and delegates to the shared core engine.
Package version should have one source of truth and support easy semantic-version bumps
a single-source semantic versioning workflow suitable for major/minor/patch bumps

ref_title: AGENTS.md - What not to change casually
excerpt: manifest schema semantics
Include a schema version in your guide and/or envelope so agents can detect breaking changes. When you must break the schema, bump the version.
prefer small, reversible scaffolding choices. leave guided TODO: markers instead of inventing product decisions.

ref_title: AGENTS.md - When to stop and leave a TODO
excerpt: Stop and leave a guided TODO: if: behavior would create a new product decision not present in PRD.md, a schema or CLI contract change would be speculative, a fixture or enterprise template assumption cannot be verified.
src/pptx_cli/cli.py owns command composition and top-level routing
src/pptx_cli/cli.py -- Typer app, command routing, envelope emission
